attributes.content:
## Overview

The Excursion card gives the workspace a structured home for activity/tour booking with time, location, provider, and notes.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Excursion acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Contact]]** -- Excursion often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Organization]]** -- Excursion frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Calendar Event]]** -- Calendar Event can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Excursion.
- **[[Task]]** -- Excursion often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Excursion can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Itinerary / Trip]]** -- Itinerary / Trip can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Excursion.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Excursion as the structured home for activity/tour booking with time, location, provider, and notes and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Excursion when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Excursion stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
